identification: 'fig' - ni is both syllabogram and fig-ideogram in LB (acrophonic principle); LA uses the same sign ideographically; Greek sukon does not begin ni-, so the source is a Minoan fig-word preserved in the gloss nikuleon (Hermonax ap. Athenaeus, Deipnosophistae 76e)
proposer_year: Neumann 1962 (gloss attributed to Hermonax in Athenaeus, Deipnosophistae 76e)
citation: S&M 2017 pp.106-107 (PDF pp.29-30 of corpus/bronze/steele_meissner_2017/chapter-6.pdf)
authority_positions: S&M 2017 p.106: 'Thus, ni serves, apart from its syllabic value, as the sign for "fig"... Linear A also uses the same sign, ni, ideographically.' p.107: 'Hermonax's nikuleon and the use of ni as the ideogram for fig in Linear B very likely are derived from this Minoan word.'
disagreement_notes: CAVEAT (mandatory): the acrophonic argument presupposes the Linear B sound value ni for the sign - the gloss anchors the LA word only if the LB value transfers to Linear A, which is the very hypothesis under test.

identification: 'sycamore fig' - HT 88.2 sequence following (and qualifying) the ideogram NI 'fig', matched to Hesychius keikune (sycamore fig) and Theognostos' proparoxytone -a forms incl. kikuna (Kanones 101.7)
proposer_year: Neumann 1960
citation: S&M 2017 p.107 (PDF p.30 of corpus/bronze/steele_meissner_2017/chapter-6.pdf)
authority_positions: S&M 2017 p.107: 'On HT 88.2 the sequence which if we apply Linear B sound values reads as ki-ki-na follows the ideogram NI "fig" and clearly qualifies them... in Hesychius' lexicon there is a gloss keikune: sukaminos.'
disagreement_notes: CAVEAT (mandatory): the chapter itself flags the dependence - the sequence 'reads as ki-ki-na' only 'if we apply Linear B sound values'; the gloss match presupposes the LB-value reading of the LA signs.

identification: 'wool' - ligature ma+ru designates 'wool' as ideogram in LB and is used the same way in LA; Greek for wool is lenos, so the word is taken as Minoan, matched to Hesiod mallos 'fleece' (Works and Days 234) and Hesychius mallukes: trikhes, assuming Minoan-u > Greek-o substitution as in the personal names
proposer_year: no earlier proposer named in the chapter - argued by Steele & Meissner 2017 from Hesiod (Works and Days 234) and Hesychius
citation: S&M 2017 p.107 (PDF p.30 of corpus/bronze/steele_meissner_2017/chapter-6.pdf)
authority_positions: S&M 2017 p.107: 'If we assume exactly what we already saw in the personal names, namely the substitution of a Minoan u with Greek o... then we have a very good match indeed. This provides further justification for reading [sign] as ni in Linear A, [sign] as ma and [sign] as ru... Thus we can add ni and ru to our grid and confirm some other entries (ma, ki, na) (Table 6.11).' Fn.23: in LB no longer a functional ligature, often ma+re or ma+ro (Nosch 2007; Petrakis 2012).
disagreement_notes: CAVEAT (mandatory): reading the ligature as ma+ru presupposes the LB values ma and ru for the constituent signs; the chapter uses the match as 'further justification for reading' the signs - anchor and value-transfer hypothesis are mutually dependent.

identification: internal LA variation places me/ma/mi in one consonant series (same consonant; vowel differs)
citation: S&M 2017 §5 p.103 (fn.18: only certain attestations listed)
authority_positions: S&M 2017 §5: (j)a-sa-sa-ra-me (IO Za 6; IO Za 12; IO Za 16; PS Za 2.2; TL Za 1b; a-sa-sa-ra-me: PK Za 11.b-c; PR Za 1.c) vs ja-sa-sa-ra-ma-na (KN Za 10a-b); i-pi-na-ma (IO Za 2.1; KO Za 1c-d; AP Za 2.1) vs i-pi-na-mi-na (PK Za 10; PK Za 11.d). No '?' marks.
disagreement_notes: CONSTRAINT, never pins: establishes same-series membership only, no phonetic value.

identification: internal LA variation places ta/te/tu in one consonant series
authority_positions: S&M 2017 §5: 'since su-ki-ri-ta is a known place name, we might guess an ethnic adjective suffix... This confirms that the ta and te signs share a consonant and differ in only the vowel'; su-ki-ri-ta (PH Wa 32) vs su-ki-ri-te-i-ja (HT Zb 158b); (j)a-di-ki-te-te (PK Za 15; PK Za 11.a-b) vs ja-di-ki-tu (IO Za 2.1). No '?' marks.
disagreement_notes: CONSTRAINT, never pins: establishes same-series membership only, no phonetic value.

identification: u and wa as reflexes of a semi-vowel (not sensu stricto one series - fn.19)
authority_positions: S&M 2017 §5: queried pair '? qe-ra2-u' (HT 1.1-2; HT 95a.4-5, b.4-5) vs '? qa-ra2-wa' (HT 86a.3); unqueried pair ja-ta-i-*88-u-ja (AP Za 1) vs a-ta-i-*88-wa-ja (IO Za 2.1; IO Za 3; IO Za 7; KO Za 1a; PK Za 12.a; SY Za 1; SU Za 2a; SY Za 3; SY Za 4; SY Za 8; TL Za 1a). Fn.19: the pair 'gives two signs that do not sensu stricto belong to the same series, but they can both be seen as reflexes of a semi-vowel'.
disagreement_notes: CONSTRAINT, never pins. QUERIED: the qe-ra2-u / qa-ra2-wa pair both carry the chapter's '?'; the *88 pair is unqueried but rests on word-initial a-/ja- fluctuation.

identification: internal LA variation tentatively places sa/si in one consonant series
citation: S&M 2017 §5 p.103; grid entry 'si?' Table 6.5 p.102
authority_positions: S&M 2017 §5: '? tu-ru-sa' (KO Za 1b-c) vs '? a-tu-ri-si-ti' (KN Zb 5) - both entries carry the chapter's '?'.
disagreement_notes: CONSTRAINT, never pins. QUERIED: both members of the pair carry the chapter's own '?' (consistent with Table 6.5 grid entry 'si?').

identification: Itanos (north of Palaikastro)
proposer_year: Younger (no earlier proposer cited on the archived page)
citation: Younger, Linear A texts §10c, archived Wayback capture corpus/bronze/younger_lineara/lineara-main.html; lexicon.html s.v. I-TI-NI-SA
authority_positions: Younger §10c: 'I-TI-NI-SA (ZA 15a.3) - Itanos (north of Palaikastro)' (no hedge on the main page). Lexicon gives only 'name in a list ZA 15a.3'.
disagreement_notes: Not in S&M's tables. Main page unhedged but lexicon does not mark it as a placename - internal inconsistency; single attestation.

identification: likely a place name (unlocated, probably south Crete); 'Knossos' very doubtful; not 'grain'
proposer_year: Hooker 1975; Duhoux 1989, 79; Schoep 2002, 148-50 (as cited by Younger §10c)
citation: Younger, Linear A texts §10c, archived Wayback capture corpus/bronze/younger_lineara/lineara-main.html; lexicon.html s.v. KU-NI-SU
authority_positions: Younger §10c: 'KU-NI-SU = likely a place name (not "grain"...); "Knossos" is very doubtful. The word appears only 3 times, and probably within the region of south Crete: as a heading without numbers (therefore a place?) on HT 10.1; as a subheading on HT 86.2 & HT 95.a.3-4 (assessments?).'
disagreement_notes: Not in S&M's tables. No accepted Greek/LB equation - classification as placename only; pins no sign values via an external name.

identification: cf. LB ku-ta-to - bare comparison only; no equation asserted
proposer_year: Younger (bare cf. on the archived page)
citation: Younger, Linear A texts §10c, archived Wayback capture corpus/bronze/younger_lineara/lineara-main.html; lexicon.html s.v. KU-TA[
authority_positions: Younger §10c: 'KU-TA[ (HT 115b) cf. Linear B ku-ta-to' (bare cf.). Lexicon: 'name in a list HT 115b.4'.
disagreement_notes: Not in S&M's tables. cf.-only and the LA form is broken (KU-TA[) - comparison, not identification.

identification: possibly Ayia Triada itself - inferential
proposer_year: Younger (inferential argument on the archived page)
citation: Younger, Linear A texts §10c, archived Wayback capture corpus/bronze/younger_lineara/lineara-main.html
authority_positions: Younger §10c (long entry): '...That SA-RA2 may be Ayia Triada itself is implied by HT 97.b where a carelessly written SA-RA2 occupies the entire side... if so, its retention at HT may reinforce the identification of HT as SA-RA2.'
disagreement_notes: Not in S&M's toponym tables (S&M fn.17 mentions sa-ra2 only as a frequent LA name/sequence). Inferential site-identification, no Greek/LB name equation - pins no sign values via an external name.

identification: placename (unlocated), known for wine & *303 - no Greek identification
proposer_year: Younger (lexicon classification)
citation: Younger, Linear A texts lexicon, archived Wayback capture corpus/bronze/younger_lineara/lexicon.html s.v. DI-NA-U
authority_positions: Younger lexicon: 'DI-NA-U placename known for VINa & *303 (see note to HT 9); heading: HT 16.1-2; placename in a list: HT 9a.3, 9b.5; HT 25a.2-3; KN Zb 27' (unhedged 'placename' but no Greek identification).
disagreement_notes: Not in S&M's tables. No Greek/LB equation - classification only; pins no sign values via an external name.

identification: first element cf. Inatos - hedged (word 2+3? of Libation Formula; segmentation uncertain)
proposer_year: Younger (lexicon comparison)
citation: Younger, Linear A texts lexicon, archived Wayback capture corpus/bronze/younger_lineara/lexicon.html s.v. I-NA-TA-I-ZU-DI-SI-KA
authority_positions: Younger lexicon: 'I-NA-TA-I-ZU-DI-SI-KA Libation Formula word 2+3? (placename [cf. Inatos], personal name (cf. DI-DI-ZA-KE) IO Za 6'.
disagreement_notes: Not in S&M's tables. Hedged cf.; segmentation of the sequence itself uncertain (word 2+3?); single attestation.
